text: The steps for partial nephrectomy were performed as previously described.
Kylar dissection of the left kidney revealed two renal artery branches supplying the lower pole, which corresponded to that seen on the pre-operative CT.
The arteries were dissected out carefully and isolated with a vessel loop.
The tumour was easily visualised and margins were then scored with diatomy.
Clamping of the previously identified segmental arteries were then done using laparoscopic mini bulldog clamps.
5-7.5 mg of indocyanin green was then administered intravenously by the anaesthetist directly after arterial clamping.
A clear demarcation of the areas of ICG uptake suggesting perfusion can be seen for normal vascularised renal parenchyma, while the ischemic tumour does not fluoresce.
Excision of the tumour then followed using cold cut scissors.
Rhinoraphy was achieved using the sliding lock technique using monoclonal sutures.
Once hemostasis was ensured, the mini bulldog clamp and vessel loop were removed and the tumour specimen was subsequently bagged.
Hylar dissection was done to reveal one segmental branch supplying the right upper pole shown previously on pre-operative CT.
Again, tumor margins were identified and scored with diatomy.
Laparoscopic mini bulldog clamps were selectively applied to the segmental branch.
Similarly, 5 mg of ICG was administered intravenously and the camera was switched to near-infrared imaging.
Areas with perfusion were shown to fluoresce, while the tumour itself remained ischemic.
Excision of the tumour with cold cut scissors was associated with limited blood loss.
Again, rhinoraphy was achieved with the sliding lock technique.
